content:
Key Findings

  • Pendle Borough Council's spending increased by £16.5M (85.5%) from 2021 to 2025.
  • The largest year-over-year increase was £10.4M (41.0%) from 2024 to 2025.
  • The only year with a decrease was 2023 to 2024, with spending falling by £2,408,615 (8.7%).
  • Total spending analysed across five years was £129.1M.
  • Spending grew by £6.6M (31.4%) from 2022 to 2023.

Year-by-Year Spending Trends

Pendle Borough Council's spending has seen significant fluctuations over the past five years. From 2021 to 2025, total expenditure increased by £16.5M, representing an 85.5% rise. The most notable increase occurred between 2024 and 2025, with spending jumping by £10.4M (41.0%). This substantial growth suggests a period of increased financial activity or investment by the council.

The only year with a decrease in spending was from 2023 to 2024, where expenditure fell by £2,408,615 (8.7%). This reduction could indicate cost-saving measures or a temporary reduction in spending needs. However, the subsequent year saw a dramatic rebound, highlighting the volatility in the council's financial trajectory.

Financial Trajectory and Implications

The data reveals a clear pattern of growth in Pendle Borough Council's spending, with the most significant increases occurring in the later years. The £6.6M (31.4%) rise from 2022 to 2023 and the £10.4M (41.0%) surge from 2024 to 2025 are particularly striking. These increases could be attributed to various factors, including inflation, increased demand for services, or strategic investments in infrastructure or programmes.

The overall change in spending from 2021 to 2025 is a substantial £16.5M (85.5%). This trend suggests that the council has been experiencing growing financial pressures or has been expanding its operations. Understanding the specific drivers behind these changes would require further analysis of the council's budget allocations and strategic priorities.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Pendle Borough Council about the specific drivers behind the significant spending increases in 2023 and 2025.
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to gain insights into the council's financial planning and decision-making processes.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see which suppliers have benefited from the increased spending and the nature of the contracts awarded.
  • Write to your local councillor to express any concerns about the council's financial trajectory and to request more transparency in future budget allocations.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and conduct your own analysis of Pendle Borough Council's financial activities.
This article is based on Pendle Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
